title: Lap-group ranker specialist blended with binary classifier
summary: The target is next-lap pit probability, but operationally only a few cars per lap are truly under strongest stop pressure.
rationale: The target is next-lap pit probability, but operationally only a few cars per lap are truly under strongest stop pressure. LightGBM’s ranking objectives are built for query-group ordering, which matches ranking drivers within the same lap context better than pure global classification. This develops the earlier ranking idea into a concrete specialist.
implementation_hint: Train an LGBMRanker or `rank_xendcg` specialist with groups = Year-Race-LapNumber and relevance = PitNextLap. Feed it the same causal features plus opponent-aware ones, then blend its out-of-fold predictions with the binary booster using rank-average or logistic stacking on held-out folds. Keep the ranker as a specialist, not a replacement.
expected_effect: Often improves ordering among contemporaneous candidates, which is exactly where ROC AUC gains can come from when the baseline already separates obvious non-stops well.
risk: Per-lap groups are small and sparse positives can make ranking unstable; a pure ranker is not probability-calibrated and must be blended carefully.
